name: Its-Not-Really-a-Pizza Pizza
recipeIngredient: Halved Black Gumdrops or Jelly Beans
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Spray a cookie sheet with nonstick cooking spray.
3. Beat butter and sugar together until creamy.
4. Add egg and vanilla extract; beat well.
5. Mix flour, oats, baking soda, and salt separately.
6. Combine the dry ingredients with the wet ingredients; mix well.
7. Spread dough onto a 10-inch pizza pan to form a circle about 1/2 inch thick.
8. Bake for 22-25 minutes or until golden brown.
9. Allow cooling on the cookie sheet for 5 minutes.
10. Carefully loosen edges of the crust with a spatula; transfer it to a plate to cool completely.
11. Spread prepared cake frosting over the cooled crust.
12. Sprinkle flaked coconut and halved black gumdrops or jelly beans over the frosting.
13. Place strawberry or cherry fruit roll-ups on top.
14. Top with green gummy worms and additional fruit roll-ups for pepperoni slices.
Note: This recipe is highly engaging for children, making it perfect for a slumber party or birthday celebration. You can prepare multiple large pizzas or several mini pizzas in advance to allow kids to decorate them as desired.
